step_rationale: Reflects fibrinolytic-anticoagulant balance; elevated PAI-1 indicates impaired thrombus dissolution.
mechanism: tPA/PAI-1 complex imbalance (excess PAI-1) suppresses fibrinolysis and promotes thrombus persistence in coronary occlusion.

references.title: Predictive value of fibrinolytic factors in coronary heart disease.
references.journal: Scandinavian journal of clinical and laboratory investigation. Supplementum
